headline: SpaceX and Other Mega IPOs May Wait Years to Join the S&P 500
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Mega-IPO candidates including SpaceX are expected to face a long road to entry to the S&P 500 Index, after the company that makes the rules rejected a proposal that included relaxing the requirement that they be profitable.Most Read from BloombergBecerra Advances for California Governor as Hilton Fights SteyerSpaceX, Other Mega IPOs Denied Fast Index Entry by S&PNasdaq 100 Sinks 5% in AI-Led Rout as Yields Climb: Markets WrapWhat to Expect From Apple’s AI, Siri and iOS 27 Launch a

headline: Tech Stocks Fall. Why Broadcom, Google, AI IPOs May Have Triggered Sell-Off.
summary: Technology stocks tumbled sharply Friday as the Nasdaq headed for its worst week in a year.  Nasdaq Inc. shed nearly 2% in afternoon trades, as Nvidia, Micron and Sandisk fell.  Tech behemoths like Amazon and Google edged lower.

headline: S&P Keeps SpaceX Waiting Despite Possible $1.8 Trillion IPO
summary: S&P will keep its 12-month seasoning rule, diverging from Nasdaq and FTSE Russell's faster index-entry approach.

headline: Did SEC-Approved QBTC Bitcoin Index Options Just Recast Nasdaq's (NDAQ) Market-Infrastructure Narrative?
summary: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission recently approved Nasdaq’s plan to list cash-settled Bitcoin index options on the Philadelphia Stock Exchange under the QBTC ticker, offering European-style contracts that settle in cash against a Bitcoin index rather than physical BTC. This framework gives institutions and sophisticated traders a regulated way to express Bitcoin views and manage risk using familiar listed options infrastructure, potentially tightening the link between traditional...
